candidates.name: Evaluation Fragility Theory
candidates.shortDefinition: Benchmark conclusions are fragile when evaluation protocols, splits, metrics, or replay conditions change.
candidates.mechanism: Protocol variables alter class exposure, group overlap, difficulty, and metric summaries before model quality is interpreted.
assumptions: Public benchmark metadata contains enough protocol signal to compare evaluation paths.
Baseline and random/stratified challengers are run before broad claims.
scope: Dataset/repo evidence where evaluation protocol affects a claim
nonExamples: Protocol-absent single-table targets with stable low deltas
Claims not tied to execution or metrics
predictions: High protocol-risk targets will show larger source-vs-random deltas than low-risk controls.
Ambiguous targets will reduce confidence even if simple execution succeeds.
falsificationTests: metric change destroys the theory's claimed mechanism
limitations: Theory Engine v0 is bounded computational theory-building, not AGI or human-level science.
No theory is called true; theories are confidence-scored and falsifiable.
Prediction tournaments use public corpus evidence and bounded execution summaries.
Cross-domain transfer is scoped and can fail or remain partial.
Public artifacts contain curated summaries and no private data.

candidates.name: Protocol Sensitivity Theory
candidates.shortDefinition: Benchmark metrics move when source-described split structure is preserved instead of replaced by convenient random splits.
candidates.mechanism: Source splits encode subject, file, spatial, class, temporal, or contributor structure that random splits can smooth away.
assumptions: Source split files or defensible approximations exist.
Comparable model families are used across split variants.
scope: Benchmarks with clear or approximated source splits
nonExamples: Targets whose source protocol is absent or not testable
measurableIndicators: consistent delta across LogisticRegression and tree baselines
predictions: Source splits remain materially different from random challengers on protocol-bearing targets.
falsificationTests: metric change destroys the theory's claimed mechanism
limitations: Theory Engine v0 is bounded computational theory-building, not AGI or human-level science.
No theory is called true; theories are confidence-scored and falsifiable.
Prediction tournaments use public corpus evidence and bounded execution summaries.
Cross-domain transfer is scoped and can fail or remain partial.
Public artifacts contain curated summaries and no private data.

candidates.name: Random-Split Inflation Theory
candidates.shortDefinition: Convenient random or stratified splits often overstate performance relative to source-described protocols.
candidates.mechanism: Randomization can mix easier examples, reduce rare-class stress, or erase source-encoded difficulty.
assumptions: Random challenger uses comparable model family and metrics
scope: Targets with measured positive source-vs-random delta
predictions: Random splits will exceed source splits on high-risk targets.
falsificationTests: metric change destroys the theory's claimed mechanism
limitations: Theory Engine v0 is bounded computational theory-building, not AGI or human-level science.
No theory is called true; theories are confidence-scored and falsifiable.
Prediction tournaments use public corpus evidence and bounded execution summaries.
Cross-domain transfer is scoped and can fail or remain partial.
Public artifacts contain curated summaries and no private data.

candidates.name: Rare-Class Metric Illusion Theory
candidates.shortDefinition: Class imbalance and metric choice can mimic protocol or leakage risk.
candidates.mechanism: Accuracy and weighted metrics can hide rare-class failure while macro-F1 exposes it.
scope: Imbalanced classification benchmarks such as Shuttle-like targets
predictions: Metric-risk checks will narrow leakage-like interpretations.
falsificationTests: metric change destroys the theory's claimed mechanism
limitations: Theory Engine v0 is bounded computational theory-building, not AGI or human-level science.
No theory is called true; theories are confidence-scored and falsifiable.
Prediction tournaments use public corpus evidence and bounded execution summaries.
Cross-domain transfer is scoped and can fail or remain partial.
Public artifacts contain curated summaries and no private data.

candidates.name: Protocol Ambiguity Barrier Theory
candidates.shortDefinition: Ambiguous protocol descriptions cap the strength of benchmark claims even when data loading and modeling succeed.
candidates.mechanism: Multiple plausible split interpretations produce materially different evidence boundaries.
assumptions: Source descriptions can be compared against execution variants
predictions: Ambiguous targets should be downgraded or deferred unless clarified.
falsificationTests: metric change destroys the theory's claimed mechanism
limitations: Theory Engine v0 is bounded computational theory-building, not AGI or human-level science.
No theory is called true; theories are confidence-scored and falsifiable.
Prediction tournaments use public corpus evidence and bounded execution summaries.
Cross-domain transfer is scoped and can fail or remain partial.
Public artifacts contain curated summaries and no private data.

candidates.name: Replay Robustness Theory
candidates.shortDefinition: Claims deserve higher confidence when replay, fresh seeds, or network-denied execution preserves the conclusion.
candidates.mechanism: Replay separates stable evidence from one-off execution artifacts.
assumptions: Replay summaries are public-safe and hash-bound
predictions: Replay failures will force confidence downgrade.
falsificationTests: metric change destroys the theory's claimed mechanism
limitations: Theory Engine v0 is bounded computational theory-building, not AGI or human-level science.
No theory is called true; theories are confidence-scored and falsifiable.
Prediction tournaments use public corpus evidence and bounded execution summaries.
Cross-domain transfer is scoped and can fail or remain partial.
Public artifacts contain curated summaries and no private data.

candidates.name: Leakage-vs-Difficulty Differentiation Theory
candidates.shortDefinition: Split deltas must be separated into leakage mechanisms, ordinary protocol difficulty, metric artifacts, or ambiguity.
candidates.mechanism: Direct overlap and feature/target checks are required before leakage is accepted.
assumptions: Leakage-risk cards or equivalent checks exist
nonExamples: Targets with no testable overlap fields
predictions: Many split deltas will not be upgraded to confirmed leakage.
falsificationTests: metric change destroys the theory's claimed mechanism
limitations: Theory Engine v0 is bounded computational theory-building, not AGI or human-level science.
No theory is called true; theories are confidence-scored and falsifiable.
Prediction tournaments use public corpus evidence and bounded execution summaries.
Cross-domain transfer is scoped and can fail or remain partial.
Public artifacts contain curated summaries and no private data.

candidates.name: Tool Value Compression Theory
candidates.shortDefinition: Custom research tools compress toward packaging, anti-hype, and replay discipline unless they beat simple baselines.
candidates.mechanism: Pandas/sklearn/manual baselines explain many raw findings, leaving custom tools valuable mainly for curation and gates.
assumptions: Tool results are compared against simple baselines
nonExamples: Tools that repeatedly discover evidence beyond baselines
predictions: Broad product tool promotion will be rejected without reuse evidence.
falsificationTests: metric change destroys the theory's claimed mechanism
limitations: Theory Engine v0 is bounded computational theory-building, not AGI or human-level science.
No theory is called true; theories are confidence-scored and falsifiable.
Prediction tournaments use public corpus evidence and bounded execution summaries.
Cross-domain transfer is scoped and can fail or remain partial.
Public artifacts contain curated summaries and no private data.
